name: Translate YouTube video titles and descriptions to multiple languages with OpenAI

options.systemMessage: You are a professional translator for YouTube metadata. You translate titles and descriptions faithfully, preserve formatting and line breaks, and you always return valid JSON only, with no extra commentary.

parameters.content:
# Translate YouTube video titles and descriptions to multiple languages with OpenAI

# 📥  [Open full documentation on Notion](https://automatisation.notion.site/Translate-YouTube-video-titles-and-descriptions-to-multiple-languages-with-OpenAI-Course-support-EN-3993d6550fd981c997cfc26dbe14d58a)

If you want to reach a wider audience, having your video titles and descriptions in multiple languages helps you connect with more viewers. This template generates the translations with OpenAI and writes them straight back to YouTube as native localizations.

## How it works
- **Configuration** groups every value you may want to change (video ID, default language, target languages, OpenAI model) in one place.
- **Fetch video information** retrieves the current title, description, category and tags from YouTube, whether the video is public or not.
- **Prepare target languages** removes the default language from the target list so you only translate what you actually need.
- **AI translator** translates the title and description into every target language and returns clean JSON.
- **Update video localization** sends the original default language plus all translations back to YouTube in a single API call.
- **Build localization URL** returns the YouTube Studio URL where you can review the result.

⚠ A translation overwrites any existing translation for the same language.

## Setup
1. (First time only) Connect the two credentials: YouTube OAuth2 and OpenAI.
2. Open **Configuration** and set `video_id` to your video ID (the part of the URL between "/" and "?si=").
3. Adjust `target_languages` and `default_language` if needed.
4. Click **Execute workflow** and review the translations in YouTube Studio.

## Requirements
- A YouTube account with a **YouTube OAuth2** credential (free tier is enough).
- An **OpenAI** credential.
- n8n Cloud or self-hosted. No paid plan required; mind the API rate limits if you extend this to many videos at once.

## Customization
- Change `openai_model` in **Configuration** to trade quality for cost.
- Add or remove languages in `target_languages` (name → language code).
- Refine the translator prompt to match your tone, but keep the JSON output structure unchanged.

## Need help customizing?
Contact me for consulting and support : [Linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/in/doctor-firass/)

# MY NEW YOUTUBE CHANNEL
👉 [Subscribe to my new YouTube channel](https://www.youtube.com/@DrFiras_AI). Here I'll share videos and Shorts with practical tutorials and FREE templates for n8n.

[![The AI Doctor](https://www.dr-firas.com/the-ai-doctor.png)](https://www.youtube.com/@DrFiras_AI)

## 1. Set your video and languages
Edit **Configuration** only: your video ID, the default language and the target languages (name → code). Nothing else is hardcoded downstream.

## 2. Fetch and prepare
Get the video metadata from YouTube, then drop the default language from the list to translate.

## 3. Translate with AI
The AI translator (OpenAI) returns one JSON object with a title and description per target language.

## 4. Write back to YouTube
Push the default language + translations back to the video, then output the YouTube Studio URL to review them.
